# OpenZeppelin Contracts for Sui

> Secure smart contract libraries written in Move for the Sui blockchain. OpenZeppelin's Web3 standards brought to Sui — a growing collection of audited Move building blocks for decentralized application development.

This file follows the [llms.txt convention](https://llmstxt.org/) to help AI integrator agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline, GitHub Copilot, Aider, Gemini CLI, and similar) **use this library in downstream Sui Move projects**. It points to maintained catalogs and per-package documentation rather than enumerating individual modules or hard-coding the current package layout — the library is actively growing, and the per-folder catalogs are always current.

## What this library gives you

The library is organized into top-level package groups, and each group has its own catalog README. **Browse these to discover what packages are available** before drilling into source. New top-level groups may be added as the library expands; the pattern (group folder + `README.md` catalog + per-package directories) stays the same.

- [contracts/](contracts/README.md): Application-level packages.
- [math/](math/README.md): Math primitive packages.

Each package within a group is a self-contained Move package with its own `Move.toml`, `Published.toml`, `sources/`, `tests/`, and `README.md`. The package's README is the authoritative documentation for what that package provides and how to use it.

## Machine-readable module metadata

Each package's `llms/` directory contains per-module YAML files describing APIs, error codes, integration patterns, and anti-patterns — designed for AI agents to ground code generation at the schema level rather than re-deriving structure from source. A module declared as `openzeppelin_<pkg>::<module>` is documented at `<package-path>/llms/<module>.yaml`, with subdirectories mirroring the package's `sources/` organization where applicable. Coverage is incremental — not every module ships a YAML yet.

## Depending on a package from your project

**Stable releases (recommended).** Audited stable releases of each package are published to the [Move Package Registry (MVR)](https://www.moveregistry.com/) under the `@openzeppelin-move` namespace — for example, [@openzeppelin-move/fixed-point-math](https://www.moveregistry.com/package/@openzeppelin-move/fixed-point-math). This is the right pin for production code. To depend on a package, add it to your `Move.toml`:

```toml
[dependencies]
openzeppelin_<pkg> = { r.mvr = "@openzeppelin-move/<pkg>" }
```

**Pre-release or unreleased work.** If you need to depend on a release tag, branch, or commit that is not yet on MVR (for example, to track an unreleased fix or run against `main`), point at this repository directly:

```toml
[dependencies.openzeppelin_<pkg>]
git = "https://github.com/OpenZeppelin/contracts-sui.git"
subdir = "<path-to-pkg>"
rev = "<release-tag-or-commit-sha>"
```

Available release tags are listed under [Releases](https://github.com/OpenZeppelin/contracts-sui/releases). The `subdir` is the package's path inside this repo (e.g., `contracts/access`). Each package's own `Move.toml` is the canonical reference for the exact dependency shape — including any transitive dependencies you would need to mirror.

The library's overall installation prerequisites — including the required Sui CLI version — are documented in the top-level [README](README.md).

## Security awareness

- [audits/](audits/): **Authoritative source for all audit information.** No audit summaries are duplicated elsewhere in this repo — if you want to know whether a specific module has been audited and what reviewers found, look here.
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): Vulnerability disclosure policy.
- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md): What changed across releases — read this when deciding whether to bump the version you have pinned.
